revision.text:
[[ja:普門寺 (藤沢市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Fumon-ji (普門寺) | image = File:普門寺本堂.jpg | caption = Fumon-ji Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|14.8|N|139|28|22|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|qid=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa, Kanagawa|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Kugenuma|ja|鵠沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma|1=Kugenuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11675779}}, Hon-Kugenuma 5-4-12 | latitude = 35| latitude_min = 20| latitude_sec = 14.8| N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 139| longitude_min = 28| longitude_sec = 22| E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | country = Japan | mountain = Mitsugon-zan (密厳山){{sfn|Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudoki Kō Kugenuma-mura Fumon-ji}} | temple_head = Henjō-in (遍照院){{sfn|Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudoki Kō Kugenuma-mura Fumon-ji}} | school = {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qid=Q3077357|lt=Kōyasan Shingon-shū|1=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}} | denomination= {{ill|Kogi Shingon-shū|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon-shū|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q134368473}}{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudoki Kō Kugenuma-mura Fumon-ji}} | honzon = {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|qid=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}} {{sfn|Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudoki Kō Kugenuma-mura Fumon-ji}} | founded = {{ill|Daiei 8|ja|大永8年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504155|1=1528}}/{{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504157|1=1528}} ({{ill|1528|en|1528|qid=Q6415|lt=1528|1=1528}}) | founder = Gon Shōzu Nagaramoto (権少都長良元) | revival = {{ill|Enpō 4|ja|延宝4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504159|1=1676}} ({{ill|1676|en|1676|qid=Q7628|lt=1676|1=1676}}) or earlier | reviver = {{ill|Zenryū|ja|善龍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenryū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ikuta Jōkō|ja|生田浄耕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuta Jōkō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{sfn|Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudoki Kō Kugenuma-mura Fumon-ji}} | formal_name = 密厳山 遍照院 普門寺
密嚴山 遍照院 普門寺 | status = Formerly one of the 32 branch temples of {{ill|Kannō-in (Fujisawa)|ja|感応院 (藤沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannō-in (Fujisawa)|1=Kannō-in Temple (Fujisawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17209747}} | pilgrimage = {{ill|Sagami Province Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage|ja|相模国準四国八十八箇所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Province Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage|1=Q135236996|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q135236996}} (47th and 88th concluding site) | website = | map_type = Japan Kanagawa | religious_affiliation= {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qid=Q3077357|1=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}} | prefecture= {{ill|Fujisawa|qid=Q386661|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}} | coordinates= {{Coord|35.33744444|N|139.47277778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Fumon-ji''' (普門寺) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qid=Q3077357|lt=Kōyasan Shingon-shū|1=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}} temple located in {{ill|Hon-Kugenuma, Fujisawa|ja|鵠沼, 本鵠沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hon-Kugenuma, Fujisawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, Japan. It is the 47th and 88th concluding site of the {{ill|Sagami Province Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage|ja|相模国準四国八十八箇所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Province Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage|1=Q135236996|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q135236996}}. From {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} ({{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}}) to {{ill|1913|en|1913|qid=Q2080|lt=1913|1=1913}} ({{ill|1913|en|1913|qid=Q2080|lt=Taisho 1|1=1913}}), it housed the predecessor of {{ill|Kugenuma Elementary School|ja|藤沢市立鵠沼小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Elementary School|1=Kugenuma Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11624538}}, known as Kugenuma Gakusha and Jinjō Kōtō Kugenuma Elementary School.See {{ill|Kugenuma Elementary School|ja|藤沢市立鵠沼小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Elementary School|1=Kugenuma Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11624538}} == History == Fumon-ji, formally named '''Mitsugon-zan Henjō-in Fumon-ji''' (密巌山遍照院普門寺), belongs to the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qid=Q3077357|lt=Kōyasan Shingon-shū|1=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}} ({{ill|Kogi Shingon-shū|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon-shū|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q134368473}}) tradition but was originally one of the 32 branch temples of {{ill|Kannō-in (Fujisawa)|ja|感応院 (藤沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannō-in (Fujisawa)|1=Kannō-in Temple (Fujisawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17209747}} in {{ill|Ōgaya, Fujisawa|ja|大鋸, 藤沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaya, Fujisawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to its temple records, it was established in {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504157|1=1528}} ({{ill|1528|en|1528|qid=Q6415|lt=1528|1=1528}}) by Ryōgen (良元), the 35th head priest of Kannō-in, at a site in {{ill|Tōdokahara|ja|唐土ヶ原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdokahara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hiratsuka|en|Hiratsuka|qid=Q502199|lt=Hiratsuka, Kanagawa|1=Hiratsuka}}, with a principal image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|qid=Q11404731|lt=Ekādaśamukha|1=Ekādaśamukha}}. In {{ill|Genna 3|ja|元和3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504160|1=1617}} ({{ill|1617|en|1617|qid=Q6798|lt=1617|1=1617}}), the monk Genchō Ajari relocated the temple to its current location at {{ill|Togamihara|ja|砥上ヶ原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togamihara|1=Togamigahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11587555}} and re-established it with {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|qid=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}} as the principal deity. The former main deity, the Eleven-Faced Kannon, was enshrined separately in a hall to the east of the temple. == Buildings == === Main Hall === The current wooden, tiled-roof Main Hall was constructed by the temple's reviver, {{ill|Ikuta Jōkō|ja|生田浄耕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuta Jōkō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in {{ill|1878|en|1878|qid=Q7776|lt=1878|1=1878}} ({{ill|1878|en|1878|qid=Q7776|lt=Meiji 11|1=1878}}), and the enshrinement ceremony was held on {{ill|May 13|en|May 13|qid=Q2560|lt=May 13|1=May 13}}. In {{ill|1982|en|1982|qid=Q2436|lt=1982|1=1982}} ({{ill|1982|en|1982|qid=Q2436|lt=Showa 57|1=1982}}), the Main Hall was renovated, and a new guest hall and priest quarters were constructed. * **Main deity** – Wooden standing statue of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|qid=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}} with two attendant child deities. * **Seated Wooden Statue of Kōbō Daishi** – Located at the 88th concluding site of the {{ill|Sagami Province Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage|ja|相模国準四国八十八箇所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Province Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage|1=Q135236996|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q135236996}}. * **Wooden Seated Statue of Amitābha** – Created by a sculptor from {{ill|Ōgaya, Kamakura|ja|扇ガ谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaya, Kamakura|1=Ōgigayatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11496826}}, Kamakura. * **Temple Bell** – Cast in {{ill|Hōreki 3|ja|宝暦3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōreki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504162|1=1753}} ({{ill|1753|en|1753|qid=Q7621|lt=1753|1=1753}}), dedicated by the believers of {{ill|Kugenuma-mura|ja|鵠沼村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma-mura|1=Kugenuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11675780}}. == External Facilities == === Cemetery for Past Head Priests === Located outside the temple grounds near the entrance of {{ill|Kugenuma Elementary School|ja|藤沢市立鵠沼小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Elementary School|1=Kugenuma Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11624538}}. === Daitō Kannon Hall === Previously located at Hon-Kugenuma 2-4-36, it now serves as a community center, housing the former principal image of Fumon-ji, a wooden Eleven-Faced Kannon. == Pilgrimage == In {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504164|1=1817}} ({{ill|1817|en|1817|qid=Q6958|lt=1817|1=1817}}), local figure Asaba Tarōemon and the monk Zenō from Fumon-ji worked to establish a pilgrimage site modeled after the {{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|en|Shikoku Pilgrimage|qid=Q1972399|lt=Shikoku 88 Temple Pilgrimage|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}}, resulting in the {{ill|Sagami Province Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage|ja|相模国準四国八十八箇所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Province Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage|1=Q135236996|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q135236996}}. Fumon-ji houses the 47th and 88th (concluding) sites of this pilgrimage. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kugenuma|ja|鵠沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma|1=Kugenuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11675779}} * {{ill|Kugenuma Elementary School|ja|藤沢市立鵠沼小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Elementary School|1=Kugenuma Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11624538}} * {{ill|List of temples in Fujisawa, Kanagawa|ja|藤沢市内の寺院一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of temples in Fujisawa, Kanagawa|1=list of Buddhist temples in Fujisawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16142509}} == Categories == [[File:普門寺本堂.jpg|260px]] {{translated page|ja|普門寺 (藤沢市)|version=100580261|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:普門寺 (藤沢市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11514632}} [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Shingon temples]] [[Category:Temples in Fujisawa, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Temples of Fudō Myōō]] [[Category:Vairocana]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
